title: Kallil Temple
tagline: Kallil Temple — spiritual town of Kerala

overview.short: Kallil Temple is a Hindu–Jain temple located at Kerala, South India.
overview.description: Kallil Temple is a Hindu–Jain temple located at Kerala, South India. It is 8 km away from Perumbavoor in Ernakulam district of Kerala. The name Kallil in Malayalam means 'in stone'. It is one of the most ancient Hindu temples in Kerala.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Mary Queen Church, Thoppil
topPlaces.description: Mary Queen Church, popularly known as Thoppil Palli, is a parish church coming under the Syro-Malabar Catholic Major Archeparchy of Ernakulam-Angamaly. It is situated along Thrikkakkara - Kakkanad road, at Thoppil, in Ernakulam district of the south Indian state of Kerala.

topPlaces.name: Odakkali
topPlaces.description: Odakkali, located at 10.09 North and 76.54 Eastis a village in the Perumbavoor Ernakulam district of Kerala State, in southern India. Odakkali is 26.8 km away from Aluva in Aluva Munnar Road.

topPlaces.name: Thrikkalathoor
topPlaces.description: Thrikkalathoor is a village in Ernakulam district of Kerala state (India), located near to the towns of Perumbavoor, Muvattupuza, Kothamangalam and Kolencherry.

topPlaces.name: Mannoor, Ernakulam
topPlaces.description: Mannoor is a village near Muvattupuzha in Irapuram, Kunnathunad, Ernakulam in the Indian state of Kerala. Mannoor is located 10 km away from Perumbavoor and 8 km from Muvattupuzha.

topPlaces.name: Aromatic and Medicinal Plants Research Station, Odakkali
topPlaces.description: Aromatic and Medicinal Plants Research Station, Odakkali is a research Station under the Central Zone of Kerala Agricultural University at Odakkali in Ernakulam district of Kerala, India. This station was established in 1951 as Lemongrass Breeding Station by the Travancore-Cochin Government.

image.alt: Aromatic and Medicinal Plants Research Station, Odakkali

topPlaces.name: Cheruvattoor
topPlaces.description: Cheruvattoor is a village in the municipality of Kothamangalam, which is located at the foot of the western Ghats in the Ernakulam district of Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Paipra
topPlaces.description: Paipra is a grama panchayat in Muvattupuzha taluk of Ernakulam district in the Indian state of Kerala. The Ernakulam district administration lists Paipra among the panchayats in Muvattupuzha taluk.

topPlaces.name: Irapuram
topPlaces.description: Airapuram is a village near Perumbavoor in the Ernakulam district of Kerala, India. It is located in the Kunnathunad taluk.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kallil Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kallil Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Kallil Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Cochin International Airport, Kochi (19 km) and the nearest railway station is Ernakulam Junction (Kochi) (30 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kallil Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kallil Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kallil Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Kallil Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Kallil Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Mary Queen Church, Thoppil, Odakkali, Thrikkalathoor, Mannoor, Ernakulam, Aromatic and Medicinal Plants Research Station, Odakkali.
